summary: Typosquat of @solana/web3.js exfiltrates host info and executes shell commands at install
details: This package masquerades as the legitimate @solana/web3.js library but ships an install.js that runs automatically via the postinstall lifecycle hook ("postinstall": "node install.js"). On install, the script collects host identifiers via os.hostname() and os.userInfo(), inspects process.platform, walks the filesystem with fs.existsSync, and posts the gathered information over HTTPS. It also invokes child_process.execSync to run platform-specific shell commands (execSync('curl...') and execSync('powershell...')) on the installer's machine. The combination of automatic execution at install time, system reconnaissance, outbound HTTP POST, and arbitrary shell execution constitutes an installer-side compromise. The package name also impersonates the well-known @solana/web3.js package, consistent with a typosquat dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
